title: MkDocs Build Lifecycle
description: Covers the local commands used to preview and publish the site.

questions.question: Which MkDocs command gives you a live preview with auto-reload?
questions.explanation: `mkdocs serve` starts a local dev server on port 8000 and reloads whenever Markdown files change.

questions.question: What does `mkdocs gh-deploy --force` do in this project?
options: Builds docs and pushes the static site to the `gh-pages` branch
Creates Docker images for every Dash app
Runs pytest before allowing a merge
questions.explanation: The command builds the site and publishes it to the `gh-pages` branch, which GitHub Pages then serves.

questions.question: Why is `mkdocs.yml` the key configuration file?
options: It declares navigation, theme, plugins, and markdown extensions
It defines the persona access rules for the Control Panel
questions.explanation: All MkDocs site settings—nav order, Material theme, search plugins, markdown extensions—are read from `mkdocs.yml`.
